name: Rio Chillar Trail
name_original: Sendero acuático Río Chillar - Los Cahorros – Parque Natural Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama
description: Hike the moderate Rio Chillar Trail, a 9.0-mile river walk near Nerja, Spain, featuring gorges, waterfalls, and natural pools.
region_intro: Hiking around Nerja offers diverse landscapes, from rugged mountains to stunning coastlines. The region is situated between two significant natural parks: Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park to the north, featuring challenging mountain terrain and peaks over 1,500 meters, and Acantilados de Maro-Cerro Gordo Natural Park along the coast, characterized by high cliffs and untouched beaches. This varied geography provides a wide range of trails suitable for different fitness levels, encompassing both inland mountain routes and scenic coastal paths. Best hiking trails around Nerja The most popular hiking route is Rio Chillar Trail, a 9.0 miles (14.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 1 minute to complete. This unique route involves walking directly in the crystal-clear riverbed, surrounded by high canyons and natural pools. Another top favourite among local hikers is Caves of Nerja - Eagle Aqueduct - Maro Gorge Cove, an easy 3.6 miles (5.9 km) path. This trail offers views of the historic Eagle Aqueduct and the coastal scenery near the Caves of Nerja. Local hikers also love the Calas de Maro — circular route, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail leading through coastal coves and cliffs, often completed in about 2 hours 5 minutes. Hiking around Nerja is defined by rugged mountains, dramatic coastal cliffs, and diverse natural park landscapes. The network offers options for both challenging mountain ascents and accessible coastal walks. The routes in Nerja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 2,000 reviews. More than 10,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Nerja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Nerja?
A: Nerja offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 180 trails available. These range from easy coastal walks to challenging mountain ascents, ensuring options for every fitness level.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking near Nerja?
A: Hiking around Nerja provides incredibly diverse terrain. You can explore rugged mountains in the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park, dramatic coastal cliffs and untouched beaches in the Acantilados de Maro-Cerro Gordo Natural Park, or even unique riverbed walks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995579249/rio-chillar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio Chillar Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there hiking options for all skill levels in Nerja?
A: Yes, Nerja caters to all skill levels. There are approximately 80 easy routes, 70 moderate trails, and over 30 difficult hikes. Whether you're looking for a gentle stroll or a strenuous climb, you'll find suitable options.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Nerja?
A: Nerja's mild climate makes it suitable for hiking year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for all types of trails. During warmer summer months, coastal walks or riverbed hikes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995579249/rio-chillar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio Chillar Trail&lt;/a&gt; (if accessible) can be refreshing, while winter provides cooler conditions ideal for more challenging mountain routes.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls or gorges?
A: Yes, the region boasts several natural features including waterfalls and gorges. You can explore areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/381716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cahorros Bajos del Río Higuerón&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/394651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Higuerón Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. The famous Río Chillar trail also features natural pools and cascades, though its current accessibility should be verified.

Q: Can I find trails that offer coastal views or lead to hidden coves?
A: Absolutely. The Acantilados de Maro-Cerro Gordo Natural Park is renowned for its coastal trails. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1243826863/calas-de-maro-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calas de Maro — circular route&lt;/a&gt; will take you along dramatic cliffs and offer stunning views of the Mediterranean Sea and secluded coves.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or interesting sights along the trails?
A: Many trails incorporate historical and natural landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995575017/caves-of-nerja-eagle-aqueduct-maro-gorge-cove" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caves of Nerja - Eagle Aqueduct - Maro Gorge Cove&lt;/a&gt; route passes by the historic Eagle Aqueduct and offers views near the impressive Nerja Caves. You can also find various castles and viewpoints in the wider area, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/311736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de San Miguel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Nerja?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1243826863/calas-de-maro-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calas de Maro — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which explores coastal coves and cliffs. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1238079810/torre-de-la-caleta-circular-por-el-cerro-caleta-acantilados-de-maro-cerro-gordo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de la Caleta circular por el Cerro Caleta&lt;/a&gt;, offering a loop through the Acantilados de Maro-Cerro Gordo.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking trails suitable for children?
A: Nerja offers numerous easy trails perfect for families. Many coastal paths within the Acantilados de Maro-Cerro Gordo Natural Park are less demanding and provide beautiful scenery without extreme climbing. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995575017/caves-of-nerja-eagle-aqueduct-maro-gorge-cove" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caves of Nerja - Eagle Aqueduct - Maro Gorge Cove&lt;/a&gt; route is an easy option that combines natural beauty with historical interest.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails around Nerja?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are generally dog-friendly, specific rules can vary within natural parks and protected areas. It's always best to check local regulations for the specific trail you plan to hike, especially regarding leash requirements and waste disposal, to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: What do other hikers appreciate most about the trails in Nerja?
A: The trails in Nerja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 2,000 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning diversity of landscapes, from the panoramic mountain views to the dramatic coastal scenery, and the opportunity to discover unique natural features like riverbeds and hidden coves.

Q: What are the parking options for popular hiking areas?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For popular spots like the Nerja Caves or access points to the Maro-Cerro Gordo Natural Park, designated parking areas are usually available, though they can fill up quickly during peak season. For mountain trails, parking might be more limited, often requiring roadside parking or small designated spots near trailheads. It's advisable to research specific trail access points beforehand.

Q: Can I reach hiking trailheads using public transport in Nerja?
A: While some trailheads, especially those closer to Nerja town or accessible villages like Maro, might be reachable by local bus services, many mountain and more remote coastal trails require private transport. It's recommended to check local bus routes and schedules if you plan to rely on public transport for your hiking adventures.

faqs.question: Where does the Rio Chillar Trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts near Nerja, Andalusia. While there isn't a designated official parking lot right at the trailhead, hikers often park in residential areas nearby. Be aware that parking can be challenging, especially during peak season, so arriving early is recommended.

faqs.question: Is the Rio Chillar Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly, but it's important to keep your dog on a leash, especially in crowded sections and to protect the natural environment. Remember that you'll be walking in water for much of the route, so ensure your dog is comfortable with this.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Rio Chillar Trail is ideal for the warm summer months in Andalusia (June to September) because you walk directly in the river, providing a natural cooling effect. It's also pleasant in late spring and early autumn. Avoid hiking after heavy rains, as water levels can rise significantly and currents can become dangerous.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees for the Rio Chillar Trail or the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Currently, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Rio Chillar Trail, which is located within the Sierras of Tejeda, Almijara and Alhama Natural Park. However, always respect the natural environment and any local regulations.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like and what should I wear for the hike?
faqs.answer: The trail begins with about 30 minutes of walking over a dried-up riverbed of gravel. Once in the river, you'll spend most of your time walking through water that is typically ankle-deep but can reach knee height. The riverbed is rocky and uneven. Sturdy, water-appropriate footwear with good grip is essential – old trainers or water shoes are often recommended. Avoid flip-flops. Bringing a change of clothes and a towel is also a good idea.

faqs.question: What are the main attractions or scenic points along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is known for its stunning natural beauty. Key highlights include walking through lush bamboo forests, impressive rock formations, and crystal-clear water. You'll encounter several waterfalls and natural pools, such as the Chíllar River Waterfall and Chillar River Waterfalls. A major attraction is the spectacular narrow gorge known as Río Chíllar Gorge (Los Cahorros), where marble walls rise 30-50 meters high and narrow to less than 2 meters wide.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: The first section of the trail, approximately 5 kilometers to the 'Los Cahorros' gorges and the first major pool, is considered easy and suitable for families with children. Many hikers choose to turn back at this point. Beyond this, the trail becomes more challenging with uneven terrain and rock climbing, making it less suitable for very young children or inexperienced hikers.

faqs.question: What makes this trail unique compared to other hikes?
faqs.answer: The most unique aspect of the Rio Chillar Trail is the immersive experience of hiking directly in the river itself. This provides a continuous opportunity to stay cool, which is particularly appealing in the hot Andalusian climate. The dramatic narrow gorges and numerous natural pools also contribute to its distinct character.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for swimming along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely! The trail features numerous natural pools and waterfalls that are perfect for swimming and cooling down. Many hikers take advantage of these spots to relax and refresh themselves during the hike.

faqs.question: Is the Rio Chillar Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Rio Chillar Trail is an out-and-back route, meaning you return along the same path. However, you can turn back at any point, making it adaptable to different fitness levels and time constraints. The full return hike to 'Vado de Los Patos' is approximately 16 kilometers.

faqs.question: What's the best strategy for hiking the Rio Chillar Trail, especially with kids?
faqs.answer: For families or those looking for an easier experience, it's best to hike the first 5 kilometers to the 'Los Cahorros' gorges and the first major pool. This section is less challenging and offers plenty of scenic beauty and opportunities for splashing in the water. Many hikers choose to turn back here, making it a manageable and enjoyable half-day adventure.

waypoints.name: Waterfall in Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara and Alhama Natural Park
